_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Evidence of cognitive decline in more than one domain.
Presence of clinical features (e.g., impaired memory, anxiety, hallucinations, aggression, etc.) which represent a change from baseline normal functioning in a middle-aged to old person.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailed history and chronicity
Admission document signed by empanelled psychiatrist

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment and need of hospitalization
Was the admission document signed by an empanelled psychiatrist?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed treatment notes submitted?
Are the following investigations done? (Complete hemogram, Thyroid function test, Biochemistry, Liver function test, VDRL, Vit. D level, Vit. B12 level, Neuroimaging (CT/MRI))
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Admission document not signed by an empanelled psychiatrist.
Clinical notes missing detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment, or need of hospitalization.
Missing one or more mandatory investigations (Complete hemogram, Thyroid function test, Biochemistry, Liver function test, VDRL, Vit. D level, Vit. B12 level, Neuroimaging (CT/MRI)).
Detailed Discharge Summary not submitted or does not mention date of follow-up.
Lack of comprehensive assessment covering changed behaviours, functional capacity, psychosocial support, and medical comorbidities.

additional_information.special_empanelment: As per the provisions of the Mental Health Act 2017
clinical_key_pointers: Dementia is a complex and variable condition
No single test will definitively diagnose dementia
The clinical features if present, should be a change from baseline normal functioning in a middle aged to old person
Assessment should aim at gathering information about changed behaviours, functional capacity, psychosocial support and medical comorbidities
History should be taken from a close caregiver, staying with the patient for a longer duration than the appearance of symptoms
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Assessment and treatment of medical comorbidities are essential components of care. These may include depression, vision/hearing deficits, gait problems, thyroid disorders, and arthritis.
referral_criteria: Not responding to adequate dose and duration of prescribed medications
Presence of red flags (Fever, Rapid progression, Seizures, Recent head injury, Alcoholism and falls)
